facts.text: Fort Dix began as Camp Dix on July 16, 1917, named for Major General John Adams Dix, a War of 1812 and Civil War veteran.
The 78th Infantry Division, known as the Lightning Division, honed its tactics at Camp Dix during World War I.
The Dix‑McGuire‑Lakehurst Museum, located on Joint Base McGuire‑Dix‑Lakehurst, showcases the intertwined histories of Fort Dix, McGuire Air Force Base, and Lakehurst.
Between 2015 and 2016, Colonel Shelley Balderson became the first female commander of Fort Dix, marking a milestone in its century-long history.
Joint Base McGuire‑Dix‑Lakehurst, formed in 2009, unites Army, Air Force, and Navy installations, creating one of the largest military bases in the U.S.
